evals.prompt: I have a bunch of changes in my repo at /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/test-repo that I need to commit. Can you help me commit them? The changes include new profile feature files, an auth token revoke function, bulk email support, and updated README docs.
evals.expected_output: The agent should detect the repo's existing conventional commit style (feat/fix with scopes), group the changes into at least 3 separate commits (profile feature, auth revoke feature, email bulk feature, and docs), present the plan to the user before committing, and use correct conventional commit format matching the repo history.
expectations: The agent runs 'git log' or 'git log --oneline' to detect existing commit conventions before writing any messages
The agent groups changes into multiple commits (at least 3), not a single commit
The profile feature files (src/models/profile.py, src/profile_api.py, tests/test_profile.py) are grouped together in one commit
The README.md docs change is in a separate 'docs:' commit, not bundled with the feature commits
The agent presents the grouping plan to the user and asks for confirmation before executing commits
All commit messages use conventional commit format matching the repo history (type(scope): description)
Commit messages are subject-line only with no body

evals.prompt: Please commit my staged changes in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/staged-repo. I've already staged what I want.
evals.expected_output: The agent should detect that only cache.py is staged (not config.py), work only with the staged file, and write a single commit for the TTL-enhanced cache implementation.
expectations: The agent runs 'git status' to assess the working tree state
The agent works only with the staged file (cache.py) and does not include config.py
The agent writes a single commit (not multiple) since only one file is staged
The commit message describes the cache TTL enhancement (feat or refactor type)
The commit message uses conventional commit format
Commit messages are subject-line only with no body

evals.prompt: Help me commit my changes in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/custom-convention-repo. There's a change to main.py that adds logging.
evals.expected_output: The agent should detect the .commitlintrc.json file which defines custom types (feature, bugfix, etc.) and sentence-case subjects, then use 'feature:' (not 'feat:') with a sentence-case description like 'Add logging to main module'.
expectations: The agent checks for .commitlintrc.json or similar convention files
The agent uses 'feature:' type (not 'feat:') matching the repo's commitlint config and history
The commit message description uses sentence-case (first word capitalized) matching the repo convention
The agent does NOT use standard conventional commit types like 'feat:' or 'fix:' which are not in this repo's config
Commit messages are subject-line only with no body

evals.prompt: I need to commit everything in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/test-repo. Just stage all the files and make one big commit called 'update stuff' - it's all related to the same sprint.
evals.expected_output: The agent should resist the user's request for a single vague commit and instead propose properly grouped commits with descriptive messages. It should explain why grouping is better but ultimately respect the user's final decision.
expectations: The agent does NOT blindly follow the instruction to make one commit called 'update stuff'
The agent proposes grouping the changes into multiple logical commits
The agent explains why separate commits are better (reviewability, revertability)
The agent uses descriptive commit messages, not 'update stuff'
The agent still presents the plan and waits for user confirmation

evals.expected_output: Even with a minimal/terse request, the agent should follow the full workflow: detect conventions, assess tree, analyze diffs, group logically, present plan, and execute. It should not shortcut to a single commit just because the request is brief.
expectations: The agent runs 'git log' to detect conventions even though the user didn't ask for it
The agent runs 'git diff' or 'git diff --cached' to analyze the actual changes
The agent groups changes into multiple commits (not one catch-all commit)
The agent presents the grouping plan before committing
All commit messages follow the repo's conventional commit style
Commit messages are subject-line only with no body

evals.prompt: Please commit my changes in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/beads-active-repo. The repo has a .beads/ directory. I've staged src/api/profile.py with a new endpoint. When you run 'bd mine', it will return: bd-42 — Add user profile endpoint (claimed).
evals.expected_output: The agent commits the staged change, checks for .beads/, runs 'bd mine' to discover the claimed task bd-42, and asks the user whether this commit closes the task or is intermediate progress. It does NOT run 'bd close' in this turn.
expectations: The agent commits the staged change with an appropriate conventional commit message
After committing, the agent checks for .beads/ directory existence before running any bd command
The agent runs 'bd mine' after confirming .beads/ exists
The agent asks the user whether the commit closes the claimed task or is intermediate progress
The question references the task id and title returned by 'bd mine'
The agent does NOT run 'bd close' in this scenario — it only asks the question and waits for user input

evals.prompt: Commit my staged changes in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/beads-no-tasks-repo. The repo has a .beads/ directory. I've staged a fix to src/utils/parser.py. When you run 'bd mine', it will return: (no claimed tasks).
evals.expected_output: The agent commits the staged change normally. It checks for .beads/, finds it exists, runs 'bd mine', finds no claimed tasks, and completes without any beads closure question or 'bd close'.
expectations: The agent commits the staged change with an appropriate conventional commit message
The agent checks for .beads/ directory and finds it exists
The agent runs 'bd mine' and finds no claimed tasks
The agent does NOT skip beads interaction without first running 'bd mine'
The agent does NOT ask any question about task closure
The agent does NOT run 'bd close'
The commit workflow completes normally without beads follow-up

evals.prompt: Commit my changes in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/no-beads-repo. I've staged a refactor of src/core/engine.py.
evals.expected_output: The agent commits the staged change normally. It checks for .beads/ in the project root, finds it does not exist, and skips all beads interaction — no 'bd mine', no closure question, no 'bd close'.
expectations: The agent commits the staged change with an appropriate conventional commit message
The agent checks for .beads/ directory in the project root and finds it does not exist
The agent does NOT run any 'bd' commands
The agent does NOT ask any question about task closure
The commit workflow completes without any beads interaction

evals.prompt: Commit my staged changes in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/beads-active-repo. The repo has a .beads/ directory. I've staged src/export/csv.py. When you run 'bd mine', it will return: bd-17 — Implement CSV export (claimed). After you ask about task closure, I'll confirm: yes, this closes the task.
evals.expected_output: The agent commits the staged change, runs 'bd mine' to discover the claimed task bd-17, asks the user whether the commit closes the task, receives confirmation, and runs 'bd close bd-17 --reason "<reason>"' with a quoted reason derived from the commit message. The question appears in the output before 'bd close' is run.
expectations: The agent commits the staged change with an appropriate conventional commit message
The agent runs 'bd mine' to discover the claimed task
The agent asks the user whether the commit closes the claimed task before running 'bd close'
After the user confirms closure, the agent runs 'bd close bd-17 --reason "<reason>"' with the reason as a quoted string
The --reason value is a brief summary derived from the commit message
The agent's question about closure appears in the output before any 'bd close' command is issued

evals.prompt: Commit my staged changes in /Users/mstruble/Software/mattstruble-skills/git-commit-workspace/beads-active-repo. The repo has a .beads/ directory. I've staged a partial change to src/auth/oauth.py. When you run 'bd mine', it will return: bd-55 — Migrate auth to OAuth2 (claimed). After you ask about task closure, I'll say: this is just intermediate progress, the task isn't done yet.
evals.expected_output: The agent commits the staged change, runs 'bd mine' to discover the claimed task bd-55, asks whether the commit closes the task, receives the user's answer that it's intermediate progress, and completes without running 'bd close'.
expectations: The agent commits the staged change with an appropriate conventional commit message
The agent runs 'bd mine' to discover the claimed task
The agent asks the user whether the commit closes the claimed task or is intermediate progress
After the user says it is intermediate progress, the agent does NOT run 'bd close'
The commit workflow completes normally after the user's answer
No 'bd close' command is executed at any point
